commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=7ae2a776478f31131abdc3d615a62ad6fe4ae7ab)
commands.review_summary:
The PR changes the web_fetch network guard wrapper to choose strict guarded fetch whenever `useEnvProxy` is enabled and `matchesNoProxy` matches the target URL.

PR surface: Source +4. Total +4 across 1 file.

Reproducibility: yes. from source for the review finding: current main already avoids `EnvHttpProxyAgent` on `NO_PROXY` matches and then uses the strict SSRF path, which still blocks localhost/private targets. I did not run a live proxy/local-service reproduction in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Manual maintainer/security review is needed because the remaining blocker is the product decision for NO_PROXY local access, with multiple linked candidate PRs already open.

target.title: fix(web-fetch): respect NO_PROXY env var when useTrustedEnvProxy is enabled
target.body:
## Summary

Respect NO_PROXY env var when useTrustedEnvProxy is enabled for web_fetch

## Changes

- `src/agents/tools/web-guarded-fetch.ts`: Add matchesNoProxy check before using env proxy

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: When `useTrustedEnvProxy` is enabled, local/internal URLs matching NO_PROXY were still routed through the HTTP proxy. Now NO_PROXY is respected so local addresses are accessed directly.

Real environment tested: ubuntu-24.04, Node 24, branch fix/issue-93807-web_fetch-useTrustedEnvProxy-ignores-NO_.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:

```bash
node -e '
const { matchesNoProxy } = require("./src/infra/net/proxy-env.js");
console.log("matchesNoProxy test:");
console.log("  localhost:8080  NO_PROXY=localhost        => " + matchesNoProxy("http://localhost:8080", {NO_PROXY: "localhost"}));
console.log("  192.168.1.1:80 NO_PROXY=localhost,192.168.* => " + matchesNoProxy("http://192.168.1.1:80", {NO_PROXY: "localhost,192.168.*"}));
console.log("  example.com:443 NO_PROXY=localhost         => " + matchesNoProxy("http://example.com:443", {NO_PROXY: "localhost"}));
'
```

Evidence after fix:

```text
matchesNoProxy test:
  localhost:8080  NO_PROXY=localhost        => true   → uses strict mode
  192.168.1.1:80 NO_PROXY=localhost,192.168.* => true   → uses strict mode
  example.com:443 NO_PROXY=localhost         => false  → uses env proxy
```

Observed result after fix: The `useProxy` guard now checks `matchesNoProxy` before enabling the trusted proxy, so NO_PROXY entries bypass the proxy and connect directly. External URLs still route through the proxy when useTrustedEnvProxy is enabled.

What was not tested: End-to-end with a live proxy server (requires a proxy setup).

Closes: #93807
target.author: ajwan8998

commands.reason: repair commands require a ClawSweeper PR or a PR opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
